name: @moneyworks/utilities
description: Type-safe utilities for MoneyWorks date handling, JSON parsing, and branded types
overview: This package provides essential utilities for working with MoneyWorks data, including branded types for type safety, date manipulation functions for the YYYYMMDD format with fluent API, period handling for YYYYMM format, and specialized JSON parsing/serialization.

exports.name: Tagged Template Literals
exports.description: Convenient tagged template literals for creating branded types with fluent APIs

items.name: d
items.description: Tagged template literal for creating YYYYMMDD dates with fluent methods. Parses various date formats and returns a proxy object with chainable date operations.

items.name: p
items.description: Tagged template literal for creating Period values (YYYYMM format). Automatically converts Date objects to periods.
items.example:
const period1 = p`202407`;           // From literal
const period2 = p`${2024}${7}`;      // From parts
const period3 = p`${new Date()}`;    // From Date

// All return Period type

items.name: ac
items.description: Tagged template literal for creating AccountCode values

exports.name: Branded Types
exports.description: Type-safe branded types that prevent accidental misuse of primitive values

items.name: YYYYMMDD
items.description: Branded type for MoneyWorks date format (YYYYMMDD). Ensures type safety when working with date strings.

items.name: YYYYMMDDWithMethods
items.description: Extended YYYYMMDD type with fluent methods for date operations. Created by the `d` tagged template.

items.name: Period
items.description: Branded type for MoneyWorks period numbers in YYYYMM format
items.example:
const period: Period = 202407 as Period;
// Represents July 2024

items.name: AccountCode
items.description: Branded type for MoneyWorks account codes
items.example:
const account: AccountCode = '1-1100' as AccountCode;
// Cash at Bank account

items.name: NameCode
items.description: Branded type for MoneyWorks name (customer/supplier) codes
items.example: const customer: NameCode = 'CUST001' as NameCode;

items.name: ProductCode
items.description: Branded type for MoneyWorks product codes
items.example: const product: ProductCode = 'WIDG-001' as ProductCode;

items.name: TransactionSeq
items.description: Branded type for MoneyWorks transaction sequence numbers
items.example: const seq: TransactionSeq = 12345 as TransactionSeq;

items.name: HHMMSS
items.description: Branded type for time strings in HHMMSS format
items.example: const time: HHMMSS = '143025' as HHMMSS; // 2:30:25 PM

items.name: ISODateTime
items.description: Branded type for ISO 8601 datetime strings
items.example: const datetime: ISODateTime = '2024-07-03T14:30:25Z' as ISODateTime;

items.name: Brand
items.signature: type Brand<T, B> = T & { readonly __brand: B }
items.description: Creates a branded type from a base type

items.name: Unbrand
items.signature: type Unbrand<T> = T extends Brand<infer U, any> ? U : T
items.description: Extracts the base type from a branded type
items.example:
type Base = Unbrand<YYYYMMDD>; // string
type Num = Unbrand<Period>;     // number

exports.name: YYYYMMDDWithMethods Methods
exports.description: Fluent methods available on dates created with the d`` template literal

items.name: gt
items.description: Check if date is greater than another

items.name: gte
items.description: Check if date is greater than or equal to another

items.name: lt
items.description: Check if date is less than another

items.name: lte
items.description: Check if date is less than or equal to another

items.name: eq
items.description: Check if date equals another

items.name: addDays
items.description: Add days to the date (negative values subtract)
items.example:
const nextWeek = d`20240703`.addDays(7);
const yesterday = today.addDays(-1);

items.name: addMonths
items.description: Add months to the date (negative values subtract)
items.example:
const nextQuarter = d`20240703`.addMonths(3);
const lastYear = today.addMonths(-12);

items.name: subtract
items.description: Get days between dates
items.example:
const daysUntilDue = dueDate.subtract(today);
console.log(`${daysUntilDue} days remaining`);

items.name: format
items.description: Format date with separator (default: '/')

items.name: toDate
items.description: Convert to JavaScript Date object
items.example:
const jsDate = d`20240703`.toDate();
// Wed Jul 03 2024 00:00:00 GMT+0000

items.name: toPeriod
items.description: Extract period (YYYYMM) as number
items.example:
const period = d`20240703`.toPeriod();
// Returns: 202407

items.name: isWeekend
items.description: Check if date falls on weekend (Saturday or Sunday)

items.name: isLeapYear
items.description: Check if the year is a leap year

exports.name: Date Validators
exports.description: Type guards and validators for date formats

items.name: isYYYYMMDD
items.description: Type guard to check if a string is in valid YYYYMMDD format. Validates date ranges including leap years.

items.name: isPeriod
items.description: Type guard that checks if a number is a valid Period (YYYYMM)

exports.name: Date Factory Functions
exports.description: Functions to create and convert date values safely

items.name: createYYYYMMDD
items.description: Create a YYYYMMDD from a string with validation. Throws an error if the input is not a valid date.

items.name: tryCreateYYYYMMDD
items.description: Creates a YYYYMMDD from a string, returns null if invalid

items.name: dateToYYYYMMDD
items.description: Converts a JavaScript Date to YYYYMMDD format
items.example:
const today = dateToYYYYMMDD(new Date());
// Returns: '20240703' as YYYYMMDD

items.name: yyyymmddToDate
items.description: Converts a YYYYMMDD to a JavaScript Date
items.example:
const date = yyyymmddToDate('20240703' as YYYYMMDD);
// Returns: Date object for July 3, 2024

items.name: parseToYYYYMMDD
items.description: Parses various date formats to YYYYMMDD. Supports ISO dates, slash format, and more.
items.example:
parseToYYYYMMDD('2024-07-03');     // ISO format
parseToYYYYMMDD('2024/07/03');     // Slash format
parseToYYYYMMDD('03/07/2024');     // AU/UK format
parseToYYYYMMDD('07/03/2024');     // US format (ambiguous)
parseToYYYYMMDD(new Date());       // Date object
parseToYYYYMMDD('20240703');       // Already YYYYMMDD

items.name: todayYYYYMMDD
items.description: Get today's date as YYYYMMDD
items.example:
const today = todayYYYYMMDD();
// Returns current date as YYYYMMDD

items.name: createPeriod
items.description: Creates a Period from year/month or YYYYMM number
items.example:
const period1 = createPeriod(2024, 7);  // July 2024
const period2 = createPeriod(202407);   // Same period

exports.name: Date Manipulation
exports.description: Functions to manipulate and compare dates

items.name: addDaysToYYYYMMDD
items.description: Adds or subtracts days from a YYYYMMDD date
items.example:
const tomorrow = addDaysToYYYYMMDD('20240703' as YYYYMMDD, 1);
const yesterday = addDaysToYYYYMMDD('20240703' as YYYYMMDD, -1);

items.name: addMonthsToYYYYMMDD
items.description: Adds or subtracts months from a YYYYMMDD date
items.example:
const nextMonth = addMonthsToYYYYMMDD('20240703' as YYYYMMDD, 1);
const lastYear = addMonthsToYYYYMMDD('20240703' as YYYYMMDD, -12);

items.name: daysBetweenYYYYMMDD
items.description: Calculates the number of days between two dates

items.name: compareYYYYMMDD
items.description: Compares two YYYYMMDD dates

items.name: isBeforeYYYYMMDD
items.description: Check if date is before another date

items.name: isAfterYYYYMMDD
items.description: Check if date is after another date

items.name: startOfMonthYYYYMMDD
items.description: Get the first day of the month for a date
items.example:
const firstDay = startOfMonthYYYYMMDD('20240715' as YYYYMMDD);
// Returns: '20240701'

items.name: endOfMonthYYYYMMDD
items.description: Get the last day of the month for a date
items.example:
const lastDay = endOfMonthYYYYMMDD('20240715' as YYYYMMDD);
// Returns: '20240731'

exports.name: Period Operations
exports.description: Functions to work with MoneyWorks periods (YYYYMM)

items.name: addPeriods
items.description: Adds or subtracts months from a period
items.example:
const nextMonth = addPeriods(202407 as Period, 1);   // 202408
const lastYear = addPeriods(202407 as Period, -12);  // 202307

items.name: periodsBetween
items.description: Calculates the number of months between two periods

items.name: isPeriodInRange
items.signature: (period: Period, start: Period, end: Period): boolean
items.description: Checks if a period is within a range

items.name: periodToDateRange
items.description: Converts a period to its date range
items.example:
const range = periodToDateRange(202407 as Period);
// Returns: { start: '20240701', end: '20240731' }

items.name: getCurrentPeriod
items.description: Get the current period (YYYYMM)
items.example:
const currentPeriod = getCurrentPeriod();
// Returns current month as Period

items.name: comparePeriods
items.description: Compare two periods

exports.name: Formatters
exports.description: Functions to format dates and periods for display

items.name: formatYYYYMMDD
items.description: Formats a YYYYMMDD date for display with custom separator
items.example:
formatYYYYMMDD('20240703' as YYYYMMDD);       // '2024/07/03'
formatYYYYMMDD('20240703' as YYYYMMDD, '-');  // '2024-07-03'
formatYYYYMMDD('20240703' as YYYYMMDD, '.');  // '2024.07.03'

items.name: formatPeriod
items.description: Formats a period for display in various formats
items.example:
formatPeriod(202407 as Period);                  // '2024/07'
formatPeriod(202407 as Period, 'YYYY-MM');      // '2024-07'
formatPeriod(202407 as Period, 'MM/YYYY');      // '07/2024'
formatPeriod(202407 as Period, 'MMM YYYY');     // 'Jul 2024'

exports.name: JSON Utilities
exports.description: Specialized JSON parsing and serialization for MoneyWorks data

items.name: parseMoneyWorksJSON
items.description: Parses JSON with MoneyWorks-specific type handling. Automatically converts date strings to YYYYMMDD and handles MoneyWorks-specific formats.

items.name: parseMoneyWorksResponse
items.description: Parse a fetch Response object with MoneyWorks conventions

items.name: stringifyMoneyWorks
items.description: Serializes data with MoneyWorks formatting rules

items.name: createTypedParser
items.description: Creates a type-safe JSON parser with field configuration

items.name: safeStringify
items.description: Safely stringifies values, handling circular references and errors

items.name: stringifyForDisplay
items.description: Format JSON for display with pretty printing and color support

items.name: stringifyForAPI
items.description: Format JSON for API transmission with MoneyWorks conventions

items.name: YYYYMMDDUtils
items.description: Namespace containing all YYYYMMDD utility functions for convenient access

items.name: PeriodUtils
items.description: Namespace containing all Period utility functions

examples.title: Working with Fluent Date API
examples.description: Use the d`` template literal for intuitive date operations

examples.title: Type-Safe Date Parsing
examples.description: Parse and validate dates from various sources

examples.title: Working with Periods
examples.description: Handle MoneyWorks periods (YYYYMM) with type safety

examples.title: JSON Parsing with Type Safety
examples.description: Parse MoneyWorks API responses with automatic type conversion

examples.title: Creating Custom Branded Types
examples.description: Extend the type system with your own branded types

importPatterns.description: Import tagged template literals for creating branded types with fluent APIs
Import utility namespaces for organized access to functions
Import the fluent date type for type annotations

designPrinciples.description: All date and period values are branded types, preventing accidental misuse and ensuring compile-time safety.
The d`` template literal returns dates with chainable methods for intuitive date operations.
Follows MoneyWorks conventions: YYYYMMDD for dates, YYYYMM for periods, preserving the native data formats.
No external dependencies, ensuring small bundle size and maximum compatibility.
Invalid values are caught at creation time, not usage time, through factory functions and type guards.
Tagged template literals and intuitive method names make the library pleasant to use.
